goal: Install ESO on the EKS cluster and create the ClusterSecretStore
summary: Installed External Secrets Operator on the dev EKS cluster via Helm with IRSA, created the external-secrets namespace, and applied the ClusterSecretStore aws-secrets-manager which reports Ready=True.
stepSummaries:
1. Create k8s/base/external-secrets/namespace.yaml and ESO install manifests [verified]
  2. Install ESO into the external-secrets namespace [verified]
  3. Create ClusterSecretStore aws-secrets-manager [verified]

reviewerRationale: All gates pass; project checks validate.

gateVerdicts.rationale: All three steps used real verification: IAM role verified via AWS CLI, ESO deployment readiness verified via kubectl, ClusterSecretStore Ready condition verified via kubectl.
gateVerdicts.evidence: Step 2: aws iam get-role returned petclinic-dev-eso-role; kubectl get deployment external-secrets readyReplicas=1. Step 3: kubectl status conditions show Ready=True, reason=Valid.

gateVerdicts.rationale: The combined output delivers the phase goal: ESO is installed and ClusterSecretStore aws-secrets-manager is Ready.
gateVerdicts.evidence: ESO deployment external-secrets has readyReplicas=1 in namespace external-secrets; ClusterSecretStore aws-secrets-manager Ready=True

gateVerdicts.rationale: Nothing deferred in this phase; ESO installation and ClusterSecretStore creation are complete in dev.
gateVerdicts.evidence: All 3 steps completed with passing gates
